rationale.R1.2: Paragraph ordering places results before methodology, and two comma splices ('the scores were aggregated...', 'the policy was thresholded...') may indicate logical/structural seams; plausibly load-bearing if the intent is a technical review.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are made (2.4 absolute WER points, 96.1% intent preservation) that can be checked against the stated benchmarks; central if a scientific soundness review is intended.
rationale.R1.4: An explicit priority claim ('to the best of our knowledge, the first streaming ASR decoder...') is asserted; verifying this against prior work could be the whole point of the review.
rationale.R1.6: The colloquial 'take a look at this paragraph' most commonly signals prose feedback; identifiable comma splices and some run-on constructions make this a plausible load-bearing dimension.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking for writing and prose feedback (grammar, style, clarity), or for a technical and scientific review (evidence quality, novelty claim, logical consistency)?
